summary: Russia launched a massive overnight drone attack on Ukraine with over 700 air attack means, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, and decoy-type drones from multiple directions; Ukrainian air defenses shot down or suppressed 189 drones, with 8 hits recorded at 7 locations. Russian forces continue intense pressure on Kostiantynivka, with urban battles ongoing and infiltration into the city from multiple directions, while the Russian MoD reported striking a UAV component production facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast. A Russian missile strike on a residential block in Dnipro killed 16 and injured 42, including 4 children, prompting a day of mourning in the city.
key_points: Russia launched over 700 air attack means overnight on 2-3 June, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, and decoy drones from Bryansk, Kursk, Millerovo, Orel, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, and Crimea; 189 were shot down or suppressed, 8 hits at 7 locations (Source: @kpszsu/63744)

Russian forces maintain intense pressure on Kostiantynivka with urban battles, infiltration from south near Illinivka and east near Stupochky/Predtechenskoye, and efforts to seize Novodmytrivka to threaten the city's northern sector; defenders report critical manpower shortages (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)

Russian missile strike on a residential block in Dnipro killed 16 (including 2 children) and injured 42 (including 4 children); a second drone strike later hit an apartment building, wounding an 8-year-old girl, bringing the day's toll to 12 dead and 37 wounded (Source: @UkraineNow/81628)
Russian MoD reported striking a Fire Point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast that produced components for long-range attack UAVs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613)

On the Slavyansk front, Russian forces are advancing along the M-03 highway toward Yurkovka (12 km from Slavyansk outskirts), with battles reported in Ray-Aleksandrovka; in Krasnaya Liman area, urban battles continue with massive UAV use by both sides (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)

In the Dnipropetrovsk region, GR Vostok is advancing in forested areas beyond the Volchya River northwest of Oleksandrihrad, consolidating and expanding a bridgehead north of the river (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)

Ukrainian spokesperson Tregubov stated Russian forces lack the capacity for simultaneous large-scale offensives across multiple front sectors and are forced to focus on local operations due to insufficient forces (Source: @UkraineNow/81621)

Zelensky stated all Russian missile types require foreign-sourced components, citing 145 components per 5 Kalibr missiles, 1,122 per 33 Iskander missiles, and over 17,000 components for 650 attack drones, calling circumvention schemes complicity in killings (Source: @UkraineNow/81633)

sections.executive_summary: Russia conducted a massive overnight drone assault on Ukraine deploying over 700 air attack means from multiple directions, marking one of the largest single-night UAV campaigns. Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 189 drones but 8 struck 7 locations, while a separate missile strike on Dnipro killed 16 and injured 42. On the ground, Russian forces intensified urban combat operations around Kostiantynivka and advanced along the Slavyansk axis, even as Ukrainian officials acknowledged Russian forces are shifting from broad offensives to localized operations due to insufficient manpower.

points: Ukrainian air defenses shot down or suppressed 189 out of 198 enemy drones (Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, and decoy 'Parodiya' types) during the overnight attack of 2-3 June, with aviation, anti-aircraft missile troops, EW units, drone units, and mobile fire groups all engaged; 8 drones hit 7 locations and debris fell at 7 additional locations (Source: @kpszsu/63744)

A Russian missile strike on a residential block in Dnipro killed 16 people including 2 children and injured 42 including 4 children; a subsequent drone strike on an apartment building wounded an 8-year-old girl, with the day's combined toll reaching 12 dead and 37 wounded; 3 June was declared a Day of Mourning in Dnipro (Source: @UkraineNow/81628)

Ukrainian forces face critical manpower shortages at the front; the Commander-in-Chief reportedly reduced infantry reinforcements to brigades under a 'fair distribution' rule limiting allocations to only 50 personnel per month, with the justification being the formation of new brigades alongside already understrength existing ones (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)

Ukrainian spokesperson Tregubov stated that Russia's current military capabilities no longer allow it to implement the plans it had at the start of the spring offensive campaign, forcing Russian forces to abandon broad offensives and concentrate on local operations across different front sectors (Source: @UkraineNow/81621)

Zelensky stated that all Russian missile types are entirely dependent on foreign-sourced components, citing specific figures: 5 Kalibr missiles require 145 components, 33 Iskander missiles require 1,122 components, and 650 attack drones of various types require over 17,000 components, calling sanction-circumvention schemes real complicity in killings (Source: @UkraineNow/81633)

Russian aerial bombs and missiles are destroying temporary Ukrainian Armed Forces deployment sites in the Kharkov and Zaporozhye regions, according to reports cited by @ukr_leaks_eng (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30633)

Estonian Prime Minister Kristen Michal admitted Ukraine can hardly guarantee its allies that Ukrainian UAVs will no longer fly into their territory, stating 'I think it's almost impossible' (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30621)

Russia launched over 700 air attack means in a single night (2-3 June), deploying 198 strike drones of types Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, and decoy 'Parodiya' drones from launch directions including Bryansk, Kursk, Millerovo, Orel, Primorsko-Akhtarsk (Russia), and Hvardiiske (temporarily occupied Crimea) (Source: @kpszsu/63699)
The Russian Ministry of Defense reported striking a Fire Point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast that produced components for long-range Ukrainian attack UAVs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613)
30 Ukrainian drones were reportedly neutralized over Leningrad Oblast, according to the regional governor as reported by TASS (Source: @tass_agency/378735)

Two Russian Emergency Situations Ministry employees were killed and two others injured while fighting a fire after a drone attack in Smolensk Oblast; a civilian was also reported wounded (Source: @tass_agency/378752)

TASS reported that drone detectors are recording Ukrainian reconnaissance UAVs over the site of a Ukrainian strike on a passenger bus in Yenakiieve, Donetsk region; the strike was characterized by Saldo as pure terror with no military rationale (Source: @tass_agency/378756)
The Russian SVR reported that EU leadership is aggressively working to expel the Russian Orthodox Church from Armenia (Source: @tass_agency/378769)
The CEO of United Aircraft Corporation Vadim Badeha told TASS that the engine for the new stage of the Su-57 fighter will begin delivery in the coming years (Source: @tass_agency/378733)

In the Krasnaya Liman area, urban battles are taking place with massive UAV use by both sides; to the northwest, battles continue on the outskirts of Sviatogorsk, with Russian Armed Forces intensifying efforts in this direction (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)

On the Slavyansk front, Russian forces are advancing along the M-03 highway toward Yurkovka, located 12 km from Slavyansk outskirts; battles are reported in Ray-Aleksandrovka, described as an important Ukrainian defensive hub (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)

In Kostiantynovka, intense urban battles continue; Russian forces are increasing the zone of control south of Illinivka and expanding infiltration into the city, with the enemy forming a advantage on the Kostiantynivka sector reinforced by continuous KAB strikes on the city (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)

Russian forces are pressing from the eastern side of Kostiantynovka in the area of Stupochky and Predtechenskoye, attempting to seize Novodmytrivka to enable further infiltration; capture of Illinivka and Novodmytrivka threatens the northern part of the city (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)
Russian aerial bombs and missiles are destroying temporary Ukrainian Armed Forces deployment sites in the Kharkov region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30633)
A reactive-type drone was tracked heading toward Kharkiv from the southern direction (Source: @kpszsu/63748)
Drones were reported on the eastern side of Kharkiv Oblast heading in the direction of Izium (Source: @kpszsu/63729)
A high threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk Oblast was reported (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67466)
One Shahed drone was tracked heading from the north toward Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67459)
Enemy tactical aviation activity was reported on the northeastern direction, with a threat of aviation strike means being used against frontline oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/63752)
Drones were tracked on the north of Zhytomyr Oblast heading westward (Source: @kpszsu/63735)
A drone was reported on the north of Chernihiv Oblast moving along the border toward Slavutych (Source: @eRadarrua/95145)
An air alert was declared in the Koriukivka district due to a reconnaissance drone near Kholmiv (Source: @eRadarrua/95132)

In the Dnipropetrovsk region, northwest of the village of Oleksandrihrad, the Russian GR Vostok is advancing in the forested area beyond the Volchya River, consolidating and expanding a bridgehead north of the river (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)
Russian aerial bombs and missiles are destroying temporary Ukrainian Armed Forces deployment sites in the Zaporozhye region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30633)
In the Zaporozhye and Kherson regions, the situation remains unchanged but the logistical situation remains difficult (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)
A group of drones from the south was tracked heading in the direction of Kryvyi Rih (Source: @kpszsu/63715)
Drones were reported on Dnipropetrovsk Oblast heading toward Solone (Source: @kpszsu/63737)
Three drones were reported over Solone and four over Synelnykove in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, likely all heading toward Dnipro (Source: @eRadarrua/95131)
A drone from Kherson Oblast was tracked heading toward/past Bereznehuvate (Mykolaiv Oblast) (Source: @kpszsu/63733)
A drone was reported on Odesa Oblast near Buialyk heading north (Source: @kpszsu/63734)
Drones were reported on the north of Rivne Oblast heading west (Source: @kpszsu/63740)

Ukraine's overnight air defense operation on 2-3 June engaged over 700 enemy air attack means using aviation, anti-aircraft missile troops, EW units, unmanned systems units, and mobile fire groups; 189 of 198 drones were shot down or suppressed across northern, southern, and eastern Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/63744)

The drone attack types included Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas strike drones and 'Parodiya' decoy-type imitator drones launched from Bryansk, Kursk, Millerovo, Orel, Primorsko-Akhtarsk (Russia), and Hvardiiske (occupied Crimea) (Source: @kpszsu/63744)
A high threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk Oblast was issued (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67466)
Enemy tactical aviation activity was detected on the northeastern axis with a threat of aviation strike means against frontline oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/63752)
Russian forces are using KAB guided bombs intensively on Kostiantynivka, with defenders reporting continuous pressure from enemy pilots and relentless infantry assaults (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)
Both sides are using UAVs massively in the Krasnaya Liman area urban battles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)
Russian MoD reported striking a Fire Point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast producing components for long-range Ukrainian attack UAVs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613)

key_developments: Russia launched over 700 air attack means overnight on 2-3 June, including 198 strike and decoy drones from multiple directions; Ukrainian air defenses shot down or suppressed 189, but 8 drones struck 7 locations with debris falling at 7 more locations across northern, southern, and eastern Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/63744)

Russian forces are intensifying urban combat in Kostiantynivka, expanding control south of Illinivka, infiltrating from the east near Stupochky and Predtechenskoye, and attempting to seize Novodmytrivka to threaten the city's northern sector, all while defenders face critical manpower shortages with reinforcements limited to 50 personnel per month per brigade (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)

A Russian missile strike on a residential block in Dnipro killed 16 including 2 children and injured 42 including 4 children; a follow-up drone strike on an apartment building wounded an 8-year-old girl, with the day's combined toll reaching 12 dead and 37 wounded, prompting a Day of Mourning on 3 June (Source: @UkraineNow/81628)

On the Slavyansk front, Russian forces are advancing along the M-03 highway toward Yurkovka (12 km from Slavyansk outskirts) with battles in Ray-Aleksandrovka; in Krasnaya Liman, urban battles continue with massive UAV use; GR Vostok is expanding a bridgehead beyond the Volchya River near Oleksandrihrad (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)

Ukrainian military spokesperson Tregubov stated Russian forces can no longer execute their original spring offensive plans due to insufficient forces, forcing a shift from simultaneous large-scale offensives across multiple sectors to concentrated local operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81621)

Zelensky stated all Russian missile production depends on foreign components, citing 145 components per 5 Kalibr missiles, 1,122 per 33 Iskander missiles, and over 17,000 for 650 attack drones, calling sanction-circumvention schemes complicity in killings (Source: @UkraineNow/81633)

A high threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk Oblast was issued; enemy tactical aviation activity was detected on the northeastern axis; 30 Ukrainian drones were neutralized over Leningrad Oblast; and two Russian EMERCOM employees were killed fighting a drone-caused fire in Smolensk Oblast (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67466)
sections.threat_assessment: The scale of Russia's overnight drone attack—over 700 air attack means in a single night—represents a significant escalation in strategic pressure on Ukrainian civilian infrastructure and air defense systems, even as ground operations shift toward localized efforts. The convergence of mass drone strikes with intensified urban combat around Kostiantynivka and advances on the Slavyansk axis suggests a Russian strategy of simultaneous attrition at strategic and operational levels. Ukrainian air defenses demonstrated strong performance (intercepting 189 of 198 drones) but the 8 successful strikes on a residential block in Dnipro causing 16 deaths highlights the lethal cost of even limited penetration. The reported reduction of infantry reinforcements to 50 personnel per brigade per month raises serious concerns about Ukraine's ability to sustain defensive positions under continuous multi-axis pressure.
sections.regional_response: Former NATO Secretary General Stoltenberg characterized the current NATO situation as more complex than in 2024 when he left office, warning that Europe is mentally unprepared for a major war despite witnessing one on its doorstep, while emphasizing the alliance's primary task of keeping North America and Europe together. Estonian Prime Minister Kristen Michal admitted Ukraine can hardly guarantee that its UAVs will not stray into allied territory, acknowledging the difficulty of containing drone operations within conflict zones. A Polish Sejm vice-speaker called for blocking Ukraine's EU accession over the controversy surrounding the Heroes of UPA unit, also urging Poland to stop paying for Starlink satellite services used by Ukrainian military.
sections.intelligence_notes: The Russian MoD's claim of striking a UAV component production facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, if confirmed, represents a targeting shift toward Ukraine's domestic drone manufacturing capacity, which has become a critical asymmetric capability. The reported 'fair distribution' rule limiting brigade reinforcements to 50 personnel per month, justified by the formation of new brigades alongside understrength existing ones, suggests a structural tension in Ukrainian force generation that may favor quantity of formations over combat effectiveness of individual units. The massive overnight drone campaign utilizing decoy-type 'Parodiya' drones alongside strike platforms indicates continued Russian adaptation in saturation tactics designed to overwhelm Ukrainian air defense networks.
